---
title: dbGaP
alias: &ALIAS dbgap
href: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gap/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Database of Genotypes and Phenotypes (dbGaP) at NCBI archives and distributes data and results from studies investigating the interaction of genotype and phenotype in humans.

## Key Features

- Hosts controlled-access human genotype and phenotype data from NIH-funded studies
- Covers GWAS, WGS, WES, and longitudinal cohort studies with linked clinical phenotypes
- Provides open-access summary statistics and metadata with controlled-access individual-level data
- Streamlined data access request process through the NIH Data Access Committee system
- Integrates with NCBI SRA for raw sequencing data associated with dbGaP studies

## Use Cases in Research

- Accessing GWAS and WGS data for infectious disease susceptibility and AMR-related phenotypes
- Performing polygenic risk score analyses using large human cohort data with phenotypic outcomes
- Linking germline genetic variation to pharmacogenomic responses to antibiotic treatment
- Analyzing human genetic determinants of microbiome composition and diversity
- Using dbGaP cohorts as validation sets for AI models trained on genomic and clinical data

## Getting Started

Visit [dbGaP](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gap/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
